not_found.name: Unknown
description: ## Error
Unable to find the requested bloodrager bloodline.

aberrant.name: Aberrant
description: ## Aberrant Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/17›
There is a taint in your blood that is both alien and bizarre. When you bloodrage, this manifests in peculiar and terrifying ways.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Combat Reflexes~Great Fortitude~Improved Disarm~Improved Grapple~Improved Initiative~Improved Unarmed Strike~Iron Will"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="enlarge person" s10="see invisibility" s13=displacement s16="black tentacles"}
### Bloodline Powers
While bloodraging, you gain the abilities and immunities of some ‹type/aberration«s›, but show signs of your tainted heritage.

::ab[Staggering Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="When you confirm a critical hit the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw or be ‹misc/staggered› for 1 round. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier. These effects stack with the ‹feat/Staggering Critical› feat; the target must save against each effect individually."}
::ab[Abnormal Reach (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="Your limbs elongate; your reach increases by 5 feet."}
::ab[Aberrant Fortitude (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You become immune to the ‹misc/sickened› and ‹misc/nauseated› conditions."}

::ab[Unusual Anatomy (Su)]{l=12 icon=def passive="Your internal anatomy shifts and changes, giving you a 50% chance to negate any critical hit or ‹ability/sneak attack› that hits you. The damage is instead rolled normally."}
::ab[Aberrant Resistance (Su)]{l=16 icon=def passive="You are immune to disease, exhaustion, fatigue, and poison, and to the staggered condition."}

::ab[Aberrant Form (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="Your body becomes truly unnatural. You are immune to critical hits and sneak attacks. In addition, you gain ‹umr/blindsight› with a range of 60 feet and your bloodrager damage reduction increases by 1. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

abyssal.name: Abyssal
description: ## Abyssal Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/17›

Generations ago, a demon spread its filth into the essence of your bloodline. While it doesn't manifest in all of your kin, in those moments when you're bloodraging, you embody its terrifying presence.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Cleave~Great Fortitude~Improved Bull Rush~Improved Sunder~Intimidating Prowess~Power Attack~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="ray of enfeeblement" s10="bull's strength" s13=rage s16=stoneskin}
### Bloodline Powers
The power of the Abyss courses through your veins, causing horrific transformations during your bloodrage.

::ab[Claws (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="You grow claws while bloodraging. These claws are treated as ‹umr/natural weapons›, allowing you to make two claw attacks as a full attack, using your full base attack bonus. These attacks deal 1d6 points of damage each (1d4 if you are Small) plus your Strength modifier." imp4="Your claws are considered magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp8="The base damage your claws deal increases to 1d8 points (1d6 if you are small)." imp12="Your claws become ‹magic-enh/flaming› weapons, which deal an additional 1d6 points of fire damage on a hit."}

::ab[Demonic Bulk (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can choose to grow one size category larger than your base size (as ‹spell/enlarge person›) even if you aren't humanoid."}
::ab[Demon Resistances (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to acid, cold, and fire. At 16th level, these resistances increase to 10."}

::ab[Abyssal Bloodrage (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="The morale bonus to Strength granted by your bloodrage increases by 2, but the penalty to AC becomes -4 instead of -2." imp16="The bonus to Strength increases by 4 instead." imp20="The bonus increases by 6 instead."}

::ab[Demonic Aura (Su)]{l=16 icon=aura ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose to exude an aura of fire. The aura is a 5-foot ‹misc/burst› centered on you, and deals 2d6 + your Constitution modifier points of fire damage to creatures that end their turns within it."}
::ab[Demonic Immunities (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You're ‹umr/immune› to electricity and poison. You have this benefit constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

aquatic.name: Aquatic
description: ## Aquatic Bloodline
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/51›
The anger in your blood rises from the ocean depths, seeded by descent from undersea empires, creeping ichthyic infiltrators into remote seaside villages, or something deeper still.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Aquadynamic Focus~Dodge~Lightning Reflexes~Mobility~Skill Focus|Fly~Steam Spell~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="hydraulic push" s10=slipstream s13="hydraulic torrent" s16="control water"}
### Bloodline Powers
::ab[Underwater Attacks (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="Your bludgeoning and slashing melee attacks underwater ignore the usual underwater penalties."}

::ab[Aquatic Adaptation (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-def passive="You gain a swim speed of 30 feet and the ability to breathe both underwater and in air." imp8="You gain cold ‹umr/resistance› 5." imp12="Your swim speed increases to 60 feet, and you have the 4th-level benefits of aquatic adaptation constantly, even when not in bloodrage (but not the 8th- and 12th-level benefits)."}
::ab[Watersense (Ex)]{icon=power l8="You gain ‹umr/tremorsense› with a range of 30 feet in water." l12="You instead gain tremorsense with a range of 60 feet when in water."}

::ab[Wavedarter (Sp, Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="Your bloodrage directs the flow of water to assist you in battle. While underwater, your reach increases by 5 feet and you gain the effects of ‹spell/haste›."}

::ab[Currentcaller (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost swift="When underwater, at the start of your turn you can call up a ‹rule/current› with a speed up to 20 feet in the direction of your choice in your space (moving with you) that lasts until the end of your turn." usage="3 times/day"}

::ab[Deep Fury (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-def passive="You gain tremorsense 120 feet in water, as well as ‹ability/evasion› and ‹umr/immunity› to cold and ‹rule/pressure damage›. You have these benefits constantly, even when not in bloodrage."}

arcane.name: Arcane
description: ## Arcane Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/18›
While others of your kin may be powerful wizards and sorcerers, the eldritch nature of the blood coursing through your veins transforms you into a spell-breaking terror.
**Bonus Feats:** ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Disruptive›\*, ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Iron Will›, ‹feat/Power Attack›, ‹feat/Quick Draw›, ‹feat/Spellbreaker›\*
Your bloodrager levels count as fighter levels for the purposes of qualifying for any feats marked with an asterisk (\*). This stacks with any levels in fighter you have.
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="magic missile" s10=invisibility s13="lightning bolt" s16="dimension door"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, arcane power transforms you into an arcane juggernaut who can cut down even the most careful caster.

::ab[Disruptive Bloodrage (Su)]{l=1 icon=lower passive="The DC to ‹rule/cast spells defensively› increases by 2 for enemies within your threatened area. This increase stacks with that granted by the ‹feat/Disruptive› feat."}

::ab[Arcane Bloodrage (Sp)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose one of the following spells and apply its effects to yourself: ‹spell/blur›, ‹spell/protection from arrows›, ‹spell/resist energy› (choose one energy type), or ‹spell/spider climb›. These effects last for as long as you continue bloodraging, regardless of the spell's normal duration."}

::ab[Greater Arcane Bloodrage (Sp)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can choose to apply the effects of either ‹spell/displacement› or ‹spell/haste› to yourself. This is in addition to *arcane bloodrage,* and otherwise works as that ability."}

::ab[Caster's Scourge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=melee ability="You gain a number of extra attacks of opportunity equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum 1). You can use these attacks of opportunity only against spellcasters in your threatened area who cast or attempt to cast defensively. The ‹feat/Spellbreaker› feat, the *caster's bane* bloodrage power, or some similar effect is still required to make attacks of opportunity against spellcasters who are casting defensively. You have this ability constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

::ab[True Arcane Bloodrage (Sp)]{l=16 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose one of the following spells and apply its effects to yourself: ‹spell/beast shape IV› (choose a creature your size or larger only), ‹spell/form of the dragon I›, or ‹spell/transformation›. This is in addition to *arcane bloodrage* and *greater arcane bloodrage,* and otherwise works as those abilities."}

::ab[Caster's Bane (Ex)]{l=20 icon=melee passive="Spellcasters with a caster level lower than your bloodrager level always provoke attacks of opportunity within your threatened area, even when casting defensively. You have this ability constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

black_blood.name: Black Blood
description: ## Black Blood Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/8›
Contact with the ‹magic-artifact/black blood of Orv› - by you or one of your ancestors - transformed your bloodline. This necromantic taint in your blood mutates you into something peculiar.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Combat Reflexes~Diehard~Endurance~Great Fortitude~Improved Initiative~Iron Will~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="chill touch" s10="unshakable chill" s13="elemental aura" s16="black tentacles"}
### Bloodline Powers
The insidious magic of black blood grants you profane powers and hideous resistances.

::ab[Black Blood-Immunity (Su)]{l=1 icon=def passive="Your blood runs black with the blood of Orv. You are immune to the effects (both beneficial and destructive) of black blood. You have this benefit constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

::ab[Black Blood-Cold (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee immediate="When you take damage from a slashing or piercing attack while bloodraging, you can grant your melee attacks the ‹magic-enh/frost› weapon special ability for 2 rounds." usage="3 times/day"}
::ab[Abnormal Reach (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="The black blood pumping within you twists and elongates your limbs. Your reach increases by 5 feet."}

::ab[Black Blood Resistance (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to cold and a +2 bonus on saving throws against ‹rule/ability drain›, death effects, disease, ‹rule/energy drain›, paralysis, and poison." imp16="This resistance increases to 10 and the saving throw bonus increases to +4."}

::ab[Retributive Spray (Su)]{l=12 icon=line passive="Whenever you're hit by an attack or spell that deals slashing or piercing damage, your black blood sprays up to 10 feet toward the opponent who attacked you, striking the first creature along its path. That creature takes 1d8 points of cold damage + 1 point of cold damage for every 2 bloodrager levels you possess. A successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier) halves the damage."}

::ab[Black Blood Transfusion (Su)]{l=16 icon=melee-lower passive="When you confirm a critical hit, some of your black blood seeps into the target's wounds. The next time the target would be healed by positive energy, the healing burns away the black blood and ends this effect, providing no other benefit. This ability is not effective against creatures that have no blood. The black blood transfusion persists until it negates one positive energy healing effect, but it can be removed sooner with a successful DC 25 Heal check."}

::ab[Black Blood Immunity (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to cold, nonlethal damage, critical hits, and ‹ability/sneak attack«s›. You have this benefit constantly, even when not bloodraging."}

celestial.name: Celestial
description: ## Celestial Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/19›
By way of a celestial ancestor or divine intervention, the blood of angels fills your body with a holy potency, granting you a majestic visage and angelic powers when you enter your bloodrage.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Dodge~Improved Initiative~Iron Will~Mobility~Mounted Combat~Ride-By Attack~Weapon Focus"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7=bless s10="resist energy" s13=heroism s16="holy smite"}
### Bloodline Powers
Your bloodline grants a number of resistances and changes your form to something angelic and terrible to behold when you bloodrage.

::ab[Angelic Attacks (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="Your melee attacks are considered good-aligned weapons for the purpose of bypassing damage reduction. Furthermore, when you deal damage with a melee attack to an evil outsider, you deal an additional 1d6 points of damage. This additional damage stacks with effects such as ‹spell/align weapon› and those granted by a weapon with the ‹magic-enh/holy› weapon special ability."}
::ab[Celestial Resistances (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to acid and cold." imp12="These resistances increase to 10."}

::ab[Conviction (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="Once per bloodrage you can reroll one ability check, skill check, or saving throw you just made. You must decide to use this ability after the die is rolled, but before the GM reveals the results. You must take the second result, even if it's worse."}
::ab[Wings of Heaven (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="You can choose to sprout feathery wings and fly with a speed of 60 feet and good maneuverability." imp20="Your fly speed increases to 80 feet."}

::ab[Angelic Protection (Su)]{l=16 icon=def passive="You gain a +4 deflection bonus to AC and a +4 resistance bonus on saving throws against attacks made or effects created by evil creatures. In addition, you are affected as if subject to a ‹spell/protection from evil› spell. This effect cannot be dispelled."}

::ab[Ascension (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You become infused with the power of the heavens. You gain ‹umr/immunity› to acid, cold, and petrification. You also gain resistance 10 to electricity and fire, as well as a +4 racial bonus on saving throws against poison. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

destined.name: Destined
description: ## Destined Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/19›
Your bloodline is destined for great things. When you bloodrage, you exude a greatness that makes all but the most legendary creatures seem lesser.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Diehard~Endurance~Improved Initiative~Intimidating Prowess~Leadership~Lightning Reflexes~Weapon Focus"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7=shield s10=blur s13="protection from energy" s16="freedom of movement"}
### Bloodline Powers
Your future greatness grants you the might to strike your enemies with awe.

::ab[Destined Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee free="You can grant yourself an insight bonus equal to 1/2 your bloodrager level (minimum 1) on one melee attack." usage="3 times/day." imp12="You can use this five times a day."}

::ab[Fated Bloodrager (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain a +1 luck bonus to AC and on saving throws." imp8="The bonus increases to +2." imp12="The bonus increases to +3." imp16="The bonus increases to +4." imp20="The bonus increases to +5."}

::ab[Certain Strike (Su)]{l=8 icon=roll ability="You may reroll an attack roll once during a bloodrage. You must decide to use this ability after the die is rolled, but before the GM reveals the results. You must take the second result, even if it's worse."}

::ab[Defy Death (Su)]{l=12 icon=def usage="Once a day" ability="When an attack or spell that deals damage would result in your death, you can attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. If you succeed, you are instead reduced to 1 hit point; if you succeed and already have less than 1 hit point, you instead take no damage."}

::ab[Unstoppable (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost-def passive="Any critical threats you score are automatically confirmed. Any critical threats made against you confirm only if the second roll results in a natural 20 (or is automatically confirmed)."}

::ab[Victory or Death (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You are immune to paralysis and petrification, as well as to the stunned, dazed, and staggered conditions. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

draconic.name: Draconic
description: ## Draconic Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/19›
At some point in your family's history, a dragon interbred with your bloodline. Now, the sublime monster's ancient power fuels your bloodrage.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Blind-Fight~Cleave~Great Fortitude~Improved Initiative~Power Attack~Skill Focus|Fly~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7=shield s10="resist energy" s13=fly s16=fear}
### Bloodline Powers

The power of dragons flows through you and manifests in a number of ways. At 1st level, you must select one of the chromatic or metallic dragon types. Once chosen, this cannot be changed. A number of your bloodline powers deal damage and grant resistances based on your dragon type, as noted below.
**Dragon:** Choose one.
| Dragon Type | Energy Type | Breath Shape |
| @ripple[family/Black<_dragon] | Acid | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Blue<_dragon] | Electricity | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Green<_dragon] | Acid | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Red<_dragon] | Fire | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/White<_dragon] | Cold | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Brass<_dragon] | Fire | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Bronze<_dragon] | Electricity | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Copper<_dragon] | Acid | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Gold<_dragon] | Fire | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Silver<_dragon] | Cold | 30-foot cone |

::ab[Claws (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="You grow claws. These claws are treated as ‹umr/natural weapons›, allowing you to make two claw attacks as a full attack, using your full base attack bonus. These attacks deal 1d6 points of damage each (1d4 if you are Small) plus your Strength modifier." imp4="Your claws are considered magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp8="The base damage your claws deal increases to 1d8 points (1d6 if you are small)." imp12="Your claws deal an additional 1d6 points of damage of your energy type on a hit."}

::ab[Draconic Resistance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 against your energy type and a +1 natural armor bonus to AC." imp8="The energy resistance increases to 10 and your natural armor bonus becomes +2." imp16="Your natural armor bonus becomes +4."}

::ab[Breath Weapon (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You gain a ‹umr/breath weapon› that you can use once per day. This breath weapon deals 1d6 points of damage of your energy type per bloodrager level. Those caught in the area of the breath can attempt a Reflex saving throw for half damage. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier. The shape of the breath weapon depends on your dragon type (as indicated on the above table)." imp16="You can use this ability twice a day." imp20="You can use this ability three times a day."}

::ab[Dragon Wings (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can choose to have leathery wings grow from your back, giving you a fly speed of 60 feet with average maneuverability." imp16="Your fly speed increases to 80 feet with good maneuverability."}

::ab[Dragon Form (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can choose to take the form of your chosen dragon type (as ‹spell/form of the dragon II›, but with average or good maneuverability, as you would gain from the dragon wings bloodrage power above)."}

::ab[Power of Wyrms (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to paralysis, sleep, and damage from your energy type. You also gain ‹umr/blindsense› with a range of 60 feet. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}
::h3[Expanded Bloodlines]{jl}
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/4›

Chromatic and metallic dragons remain the most common dragons to impart their legacy onto other species. The bloodrager and sorcerer classes each include a draconic bloodline that assumes the character's lineage is somehow connected with the power of a chromatic or metallic dragon. Presented below are expanded options for the existing draconic bloodlines, increasing the selection of heritages to include numerous new true dragons. Each of the draconic bloodlines below alters the bloodrager's or sorcerer's bloodline arcana.

**Energy Types:** Several of these new draconic bloodline options include non-standard damage types. For the sake of game balance, many of the damage types available to the bloodlines below somewhat differ from those produced by the parent dragon. Specifically, force energy, negative energy, and sonic energy have been altered to different types of energy. In cases where a dragon's energy type is listed as physical damage (such as bludgeoning or piercing), use that damage type when determining your breath weapon damage. For the purpose of the dragon resistance bloodline power, you gain DR equal to half the energy resistance you would normally gain against all attacks of that specific damage type. A character with the power of wyrms bloodline power gains immunity to her bloodline's damage type. For all other purposes, such as archetypes, bloodline powers, and feats, treat that dragon's energy type as fire.
::h3[Esoteric Dragons]{jl}

Though many of the enigmatic esoteric dragons remain aloof or intentionally distant from mortal affairs, occult dragons are the most likely to imprint their bloodline onto humanoids. Often such draconic blood is the result of ongoing experimentation. Scions of other esoteric dragons are far more unusual; such bloodlines usually result from explorers traveling the realms where these distant dragons dwell and being exposed to some spectacular and ill-understood power.
| Dragon Type | Energy Type | Breath Shape |
| @ripple[monster/adult_>Astral<_dragon] | Bludgeoning | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[monster/adult_>Dream<_dragon] | Electricity | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[monster/adult_>Etheric<_dragon] | Bludgeoning | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[monster/adult_>Nightmare<_dragon] | Acid | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[monster/adult_>Occult<_dragon] | Cold or Fire\* | 30-foot cone |
\*Choose one when the bloodline is first selected
::h3[Imperial Dragons]{jl}

The blood of imperial dragons is well known throughout the lands of Tian Xia, where such dragons form a strong part of much of the continent's mythology. This is particularly true in Xa Hoi, where imperial dragons rule. Imperial dragons in other lands may vary in their interactions with humanoids, but universally accept the praise (and fear) of those around them. Their connection to the land makes the blood of these dragons a powerful arcane catalyst in the making of heroes.
| Dragon Type | Energy Type | Breath Shape |
| @ripple[family/Forest<_dragon] | Piercing | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Sea<_dragon] | Fire | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Sky<_dragon] | Electricity | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Sovereign<_dragon] | Bludgeoning | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Underworld<_dragon] | Fire | 60-foot line |
::h3[Outer Dragons]{jl}

The interests of outer dragons take them across the cosmos. Strange devices or experimentation are most likely to lead to the addition of outer dragon blood into a world's ecosystem. The hidden blood of an outer dragon in a family's lineage is difficult to detect or stamp out. On Golarion, these dragons most commonly find themselves drawn to the fabled land of Numeria and its alien technology, though they usually refuse to share any of their insights with that nation's residents.
| Dragon Type | Energy Type | Breath Shape |
| @ripple[family/Lunar<_dragon] | Cold | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Solar<_dragon] | Fire | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Time<_dragon] | Electricity | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Void<_dragon] | Cold | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Vortex<_dragon] | Fire | 30-foot cone |
::h3[Primal Dragons]{jl}

Born from the elemental planes or the Shadow Plane, primal dragons are infused with primeval power. These dragons traveled to the Material Plane in ages past, and many of their kind continue to spread their blood and magical influence among the humanoid races of the world. Even the malign umbral dragons prosper in the caverns of the Darklands and the mountains of Nidal. Any exposure to or experimentation with primal magics may introduce primal dragon blood into a lineage.
| Dragon Type | Energy Type | Breath Shape |
| @ripple[family/Brine<_dragon] | Acid | 60-foot line |
| @ripple[family/Cloud<_dragon] | Electricity | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Crystal<_dragon] | Piercing | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Magma<_dragon] | Fire | 30-foot cone |
| @ripple[family/Umbral<_dragon] | Cold | 30-foot cone |

elemental.name: Elemental
description: ## Elemental Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/20›

The power of the elements resides in you, and at times you can hardly control its fury. This influence comes either from an elemental outsider in your family history or from a moment when you or your ancestors were exposed to a powerful elemental force or cataclysm.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Cleave~Dodge~Great Fortitude~Improved Initiative~Lightning Reflexes~Power Attack~Weapon Focus"}

::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="burning hands~ &#42&" s10="scorching ray~ &#42&" s13="protection from energy" s16="elemental body I" note="Spells marked with an asterisk (&#42&) always deal a type of energy damage determined by your element, regardless of the spell's normal effects. In addition, the subtype of these spells changes to match the energy type of your element."}
### Bloodline Powers

One of the four elements infuses your being, and you can draw upon its power while bloodraging. At first level, you must select an element: air, earth, fire, or water. Once chosen, this cannot be changed. A number of your abilities deal damage and grant resistances based on your element, as noted below.
**Element:** Choose one.
| Element | Energy Type | Elemental Movement |
| Air | Electricity | Fly 60 feet (good) |
| Earth | Acid | Burrow 30 feet |
| Fire | Fire | +30 feet base land speed |
| Water | Cold | Swim 60 feet |

::ab[Elemental Strikes (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee swift="You can imbue your melee attacks with elemental energy. For 1 round, your melee attacks deal 1d6 points of additional damage of your energy type." usage="3 times/day." imp8="You can now use this five times a day." imp20="All of your melee attacks deal this damage, and this ability no longer requires a swift action to activate."}
::ab[Elemental Resistance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain energy ‹umr/resistance› 10 against your energy type."}
::ab[Elemental Movement (Su)]{l=8 icon=power passive="You gain a special movement type or bonus based on your chosen element, as indicated on the above table."}

::ab[Power of the Elements (Su)]{l=12 icon=lower passive="The energy damage done by your *elemental strikes* bloodline ability bypasses resistance to that energy type, and still deals half damage to creatures with ‹umr/immunity› to energy of that type."}
::ab[Elemental Form (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="When entering a bloodrage you can take an elemental form as ‹spell/elemental body IV›." usage="Once a day."}

::ab[Elemental Body (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="Elemental power surges through your body. You gain immunity to ‹ability/sneak attack«s›, critical hits, and damage from your energy type. You have this benefit constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

fey.name: Fey
description: ## Fey Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/21›
One of your ancestors was ‹type/fey›, or the fey realm somehow intermixed with your bloodline. It affects your bloodrage in tricky and surprising ways.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Combat Reflexes~Dodge~Improved Initiative~Lightning Reflexes~Mobility~Step Up~Intimidating Prowess"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7=entangle s10="hideous laughter" s13=haste s16=confusion}
### Bloodline Powers
The power of the natural world saturates your being, and manifests itself when you bloodrage.

::ab[Confusing Critical (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="Fey power courses through your attacks. Each time you confirm a critical hit, the target must succeed at a Will saving throw or be ‹misc/confused› for 1 round. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier. This is a mind-affecting compulsion effect."}

::ab[Leaping Charger (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="You are able to leap over overgrowth and similar impediments with ease while charging. When you ‹rule/charge›, you ignore ‹rule/difficult terrain› (but not other creatures). You can move through it at normal speed, and it does not impede your charge."}
::ab[Blurring Movement (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="You become a blur of motion when you move. As long as you move at least 10 feet, you gain the effects of ‹spell/blur› for 1 round."}
::ab[Quickling Bloodrage (Sp)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="While bloodraging you're treated as if you are under the effects of *haste.*"}
:::ab{l=16 title="One with Nature (Su)" icon=magic-def usage="Three times a day." action="Move-Equivalent Action"}
You can transport yourself from one tree to another tree, as ‹spell/tree stride› with half the transport range.
Creatures of the animal or plant types don't attack you unless compelled to do so by magic or you attack them first.
You gain these abilities even while not bloodraging.

::ab[Fury of the Fey (Su)]{l=20 icon=lower ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose one type of creature (and subtype for humanoids or outsiders) that can be affected by the ‹magic-enh/bane› weapon special ability. All of your melee attacks are considered to have *bane* against that type. This ability doesn't stack with other forms of *bane.*"}

hag.name: Hag
description: ## Hag Bloodline
‹SOURCE Blood of the Coven/27›

‹family/Hag«s› interbreed with other races frequently, and those children who escape their grasp may pass their heritage on to later generations before it reemerges. The potent humors that accompany hag blood are better suited to a bloodrager's ferocity than to traditional spellcasting.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Blind-Fight~Deceitful~Great Fortitude~Improved Natural Attack~Intimidating Prowess~Mother's Gift"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="chill touch" s10="blindness/deafness" s13="bestow curse" s16="charm monster"}
### Bloodline Powers
While bloodraging, you manifest the physical power and arcane corruption of your hag ancestor.

::ab[Evil Eye (Su)]{l=1 icon=lower standard="You can curse an opponent with a wicked glance, afflicting one target within 30 feet that you can see, causing it to take a -2 penalty to AC and on attack rolls for 1 round per bloodrager level or until the target hits you with an attack. The target can negate this effect with a successful Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half your bloodrager level + your Charisma modifier)." useMod3=Cha}
::ab[Hag Fortitude (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You become immune to mundane and magical diseases." imp8="You also become immune to poison."}

::ab[Hag Transformation (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="Your form grows more twisted and powerful while bloodraging, but clumsy, blind aggression clouds your mind. Your morale bonus to Strength increases by 2, and you gain low-light vision and darkvision (60 feet), but you take a -2 penalty to both Dexterity and Wisdom for the duration of your bloodrage." imp16="You apply your morale bonus on Will saving throws while bloodraging to Fortitude and Reflex saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities."}

::ab[Covenguard (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic usage="3 times/day." standard="You can cast *bestow curse* as a spell-like ability. You count as a hag for the purposes of joining a coven; the coven must contain at least one hag."}
:::ab{l=20 icon=def-melee title="Curse Conduit (Su)" action=Ability}
You gain immunity to negative energy damage and all spells with the curse descriptor.
Whenever you confirm a critical hit with a melee weapon, you can immediately target your opponent with a *bestow curse* spell or spell-like ability as a free action.

infernal.name: Infernal
description: ## Infernal Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/21›
The Pit lives in your blood. Maybe one of your ancestors was seduced by the powers of ‹rule/Hell› or made a deal with a ‹family/devil›. Either way, its corruption seethes within your lineage.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Blind-Fight~Combat Reflexes~Deceitful~Improved Disarm~Improved Sunder~Intimidating Prowess~Iron Will"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="protection from good" s10="scorching ray" s13=suggestion s16="fire shield"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, hellfire infuses your blood with terrifying effects.

::ab[Hellfire Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee usage="3 times/day." swift="You can infuse your attacks with hellfire. When you do, your melee attacks gain the ‹magic-enh/flaming› weapon special ability for 1 round." imp12="You can use this ability five times per day, and the effect is treated as ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›."}

::ab[Infernal Resistance (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain fire ‹umr/resistance› 5, as well as a +2 bonus on saving throws against poison." imp8="Your fire resistance increases to 10, and the bonus on saving throws against poison increases to +4."}
::ab[Diabolical Arrogance (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws against enchantment and fear effects."}

::ab[Dark Wings (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose to have bat-like wings grow from your back, giving you a fly speed of 60 feet with average maneuverability." imp16="Your fly speed increases to 80 feet with good maneuverability."}

::ab[Hellfire Charge (Su)]{l=16 icon=melee passive="When you ‹rule/charge› the attack you make at the end of the charge gains the benefit of your *hellfire strike* without expending a use of it. If you already have *hellfire strike* active, the fire damage ignores fire resistance of 10 or lower."}

::ab[Fiend of the Pit (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to fire and poison. You also gain resistance 10 to acid and cold, and gain the ‹umr/see in darkness› ability. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

kyton.name: Kyton
description: ## Kyton Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Origins/9›

In the shadowy land of Nidal, the influence of ‹faith/Zon-Kuthon› and the Midnight Lord's favored servants - the ‹family/kyton«s› - taints the land and its inhabitants. Just as an eclipse drives animals wild, so too does the darkness unleash a madness from deep within you.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Alertness~Blind-Fight~Exotic Weapon Proficiency|spiked chain~Great Fortitude~Improved Dirty Trick~Iron Will~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="persuasive goad" s10="pain strike" s13="deeper darkness" s16=fear}
### Bloodline Powers
The sadistic power of the kytons flows through you, twisting you into a creature of pain and darkness when you bloodrage.

::ab[Painful Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="When you confirm a critical hit, the target is ‹misc/sickened› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your bloodrager level (minimum 1). During this time, the creature must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC = 15 + the spell's level) in order to cast a spell."}

::ab[Grasping Chains (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost-power passive="Chains cover your body and extend from your arms, grasping at the air around you and granting you a +4 bonus on Climb skill checks." imp9="The bonus increases to +8 and you gain a Climb speed equal to 1/2 your base speed."}

::ab[Armor of Chains (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to cold and the chains covering your body provide a +4 armor bonus to AC with no armor check penalty, maximum Dexterity bonus, or additional weight." imp16="The resistance increases to 10 and the armor bonus increases to +8."}

::ab[Agony's Embrace (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="Whenever you're hit by a critical hit, your morale bonus to Strength from your bloodrage increases by 2 for 1d6 rounds or until your rage ends." imp16="The bonus to Strength instead increases by 4." imp20="The bonus to Strength instead increases by +6."}

::ab[Unnerving Gaze (Su)]{l=16 icon=lower passive="Your foes begin to see flashes of the faces of dead loved ones when they look at your face. You gain a ‹umr/gaze› attack with a range of 30 feet. All foes who fail a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Charisma modifier) become ‹misc/shaken› for 1d3 rounds."}
::ab[Kyton Immunities (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to cold and DR 10/good and silver. You have this benefit constantly, even when not bloodraging."}

martyred.name: Martyred
description: ## Martyred Bloodline
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/19›

One of your ancestors paid the ultimate price for her beliefs. This distant relative martyred herself out of a devout dedication to some specific cause, and that sacrifice has infused you with power that you can use for good - or for ill. When you bloodrage, an inspiring fury conjures echoes of your forebear's incredible determination and selfless dedication, both offering you protection and amplifying your ability to punish any who dare incur your wrath.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Diehard~Endurance~Heroic Defiance~Heroic Recovery~Leadership~Persuasive~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="endure elements" s10="surmount affliction" s13=heroism s16="blessing of fervor"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, you channel the power of your ancestor's self-sacrifice to urge yourself and your allies on to greater feats of strength than would otherwise be possible.

::ab[Ancestral Strikes (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee usage="3 times/day." swift="You can imbue your melee attacks with a measure of your ancestor's power. For 1 round, your melee attacks deal 1d6 additional points of good-aligned damage if you are good, or they deal 1d6 additional points of evil-aligned damage if you are evil. If you are neutral, you must choose which type of damage this ability deals when you choose this bloodline, and the decision cannot be changed later." imp8="You can use this ability up to five times per day." imp20="All your melee attacks deal this additional damage, and you don't need to activate this ability."}

::ab[Martyr's Resistances (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain fire ‹umr/resistance› 5 and you gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against fear and pain effects." imp8="Your fire resistance increases to 10, and the bonus on saving throws against fear and pain effects increases to +4."}

::ab[Forebear's Reserves (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="You can reroll a saving throw once during a bloodrage. You must decide to use this ability after the die is rolled but before the GM reveals the result. You must take the second result, even if it's worse."}

::ab[Ancestral Champion (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee passive="When your *ancestral strikes* target a creature whose alignment is opposite to the type of damage the strikes deal, your ancestral strikes instead deal 2d6 additional points of damage of that type."}

::ab[Sacrificial Exchange (Su)]{l=16 icon=protect usage="Once a day." swift="While you are bloodraging, you can take a -2 penalty to Armor Class to grant one ally within 30 feet a +4 morale bonus to one ability score. (This penalty to your AC stacks with the penalty from your bloodrage.) The penalty and bonus last for the duration of your bloodrage." imp20="The penalty this ability imposes changes to -4, and the ally's bonus increases to +6."}

::ab[Eternal Martyr (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="Your ancestor's act of martyrdom infuses your spirit. Your ancestor's sacrifice transcends time and space, keeping you alive when you otherwise shouldn't be and sanctifying your form. You become immune to death effects. Material components for spells and effects to bring you back to life (such as ‹spell/raise dead› or ‹spell/resurrection›) cost half as much as normal. Your body cannot be turned into an undead creature, as though you were affected by a permanent ‹spell/hallow› effect (caster level = your bloodrager level). You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

medusa.name: Medusa
description: ## Medusa Bloodline
‹SOURCE Heroes of Golarion/16›

No magical creature is more iconic in Iblydan lore than the ‹monster/medusa›. Perhaps your lineage traces back to a medusa, or an ancestor survived a medusa's petrifying gaze and was forever changed by the experience. However the medusa's influence entered your bloodline, when you bloodrage, you gain fearsome and ancient powers.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Alertness~Blind-Fight~Great Fortitude~Improved Initiative~Improved Unarmed Strike~Intimidating Prowess~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="cause fear" s10="resist energy" s13="hold person" s16=stoneskin}
### Bloodline Powers
The cold fury of your cursed rage can freeze others in their tracks.

::ab[Gaze (Su)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can harness the power of the medusa through your gaze. Choose a creature within your line of sight who is within 30 feet of you. The creature must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw or have its speed halved for a number of rounds equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round). The DC of this save is equal to 10 + half your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier."}

::ab[Gift of the Ancients (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain a +2 resistance bonus on saving throws against ‹umr/gaze› attacks and to resist poison. You also gain a +2 competence bonus on Perception checks." imp8="These bonuses increase to +4."}
::ab[Staggering Gaze (Su)]{l=8 icon=zap passive="When a creature is affected by your *gaze* bloodline power, it is ‹misc/staggered› in addition to being slowed."}

::ab[Viper's Touch (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee passive="You grow two sets of venomous claws resembling the teeth of a serpent. These claws are primary ‹umr/natural attacks› that deal 1d8 points of damage (1d6 if you are Small). The claws also gain the following poison ability."}
::aff[Viper's Venom]{iconP type=Claw-injury dcF dcLev=bloodrager dcYou dcMod=Con freqR=4 effStr="1d3" cure1}

::ab[Stone Resistance (Su)]{l=16 icon=def passive="You gain acid ‹umr/resistance› 10 and are immune to disease, poison, and the ‹misc/sickened› and staggered conditions. You also cannot be ‹rule/flanked›."}

::ab[True Petrification (Su)]{l=20 icon=zap standard="You can use your *gaze* bloodline ability to permanently turn a creature to stone, as ‹spell/flesh to stone›. You can still use the less powerful versions of your *gaze* if you do not wish to turn the target to stone, but you must declare this at the time of the attack."}

naga.name: Naga
description: ## Naga Bloodline
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/16›
The blood of the powerful, serpentine aberrations known as nagas runs through your veins, fueling your bloodrage.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Alertness~Combat Casting~Combat Reflexes~Dodge~Lightning Reflexes~Power Attack~Stealthy"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="ray of enfeeblement" s10=invisibility s13="lightning bolt" s16=poison}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, arcane power surges through your body, infusing you with the aberrant power and terrible strength of the ‹family/naga«s›.

::ab[Serpent Fangs (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="You grow serpentine fangs. These fangs are treated as a natural weapon, granting you a bite attack as a primary ‹umr/natural weapon<s›. This attack deals damage equal to 1d8 (1d6 if you are Small) + your Strength modifier." imp4="Your fangs are considered magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp8="The damage increases to 1d10 (1d8 if you are Small) + your Strength modifier."}
::ab[Serpentine Swim (Sp)]{l=4 icon=power passive="You can swim sinuously, like a snake. You gain a swim speed equal to your base speed."}

::ab[Naga Defenses (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="When entering a bloodrage, you gain a +2 enhancement bonus to your natural armor bonus and a +4 bonus on saves against poison." imp16="The bonus to your natural armor increases to +4 and the bonus on saves against poison increases to +8." imp20="The bonus to your natural armor increases to +6, and you gain immunity to poison."}
::ab[Poison Fangs (Su)]{l=12 icon=power passive="You gain the ‹umr/poison› special attack, tied to your bite attack. Your poison is below."}
::aff{iconP type=Bite-injury dcF dcLev=bloodrager dcMod=Con dcYou freqR=6 effCon="1d2" cure1}
::ab[Naga Form (Sp)]{l=16 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can assume the form of a naga (as per ‹spell/naga shape III›)."}

::ab[Naga Thoughts (Sp, Su)]{l=20 icon=def-power passive="When entering a bloodrage, you become immune to charm effects and all forms of mind reading, you gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against other mind-affecting effects, you gain the effects of the spell ‹spell/see invisibility›, and you can use ‹spell/detect thoughts› at will."}

phoenix.name: Phoenix
description: ## Phoenix Bloodline
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #144: Midwives to Death/82›

One of your ancestors may have witnessed the fiery resurrection of a ‹monster/phoenix› or been healed by the grace of this legendary bird. Whatever the case, the flames of the phoenix burn brightly within your soul, filling you with an inextinguishable vitality that can withstand the most harrowing of assaults. When you bloodrage, vibrant energy boils forth from beneath your skin, granting you both the soothing warmth to heal a friend's wounds and the brutal power to burn flesh from bone. Your rage is an awesome and terrible thing to behold, as the raw power of your untamed life force can allow you to pull yourself back from the grasp of death itself.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Combat Reflexes~Critical Focus~Diehard~Dodge~Endurance~Improved Initiative~Mobility"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="burning hands" s10="lesser restoration" s13="cure serious wounds" s16="fire shield"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, you channel the molten fires of the ‹monster/phoenix› to sear your foes and fill your allies with an unquenchable vigor.

::ab[Dispelling Strikes (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee usage="No limits on usage, except a creature can only be targeted once per day." ability="When you confirm a critical hit against a target, you can also attempt to dispel the target as if you had cast ‹spell/dispel magic› as a bloodrager spell and used the targeted dispel function. You can dispel only a single magical effect per use of this ability." imp8="You gain a +2 bonus on your dispel check when using this ability." imp20="You can attempt to dispel all magical effects on your target when using this ability."}

::ab[Heart of Fire (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain fire ‹umr/resistance› 5. Whenever you are subjected to a magical healing effect from a ‹main/cure spell<s›, you regain 1 additional hit point per die rolled." imp8="Your fire resistance increases to 10, and you regain 2 additional hit points per die rolled when you are healed by a *cure* spell."}

::ab[Blazing Vitality (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura passive="When tensions run high and your emotions flare, you let forth waves of restorative energy from within. You emit a 10-foot-radius aura of energizing fire while bloodraging. Any ally that ends their turn within this aura gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to your Constitution modifier. These temporary hit points last for 1 minute."}

::ab[Molten Wings (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="When entering a bloodrage you can choose to have wings of flame sprout from your back, giving you a fly speed of 60 feet with average maneuverability." imp16="Your fly speed increases to 80 feet with good maneuverability."}

::ab[Self-Resurrection (Su)]{l=16 icon=def passive="From the ashes of your body springs forth new life. Once per day when you are reduced below 0 hit points while you are bloodraging, you can call upon the power in your blood to pull yourself back from death. This functions as a ‹spell/breath of life› spell cast upon yourself. Using this ability does not take an action. This ability does not function if your body is completely destroyed by an effect such as ‹spell/disintegrate›." imp20="This instead functions as a ‹spell/heal› spell, except that the healing can return you to life as per *breath of life,* using your bloodrager level as your caster level."}

::ab[Phoenix Fire (Su)]{l=20 icon=melee-aura-def passive="The power of the phoenix brings righteous destruction to any who oppose it. While bloodraging, you gain the following effects: your melee attacks deal an additional 2d6 points of fire damage, any enemies within 20 feet of you must succeed at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier) or take 4d6 points of fire damage at the start of their turn, and any creature that attacks you with a natural or non-reach weapon takes 1d6 points of fire damage (no save) with each successful hit."}

salamander.name: Salamander
description: ## Salamander Bloodline
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/5›
Salamanders are fierce warriors and master smiths, and you wield their might and mastery of metal by birthright.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Cleave~Improved Grapple~Improved Iron Will~Iron Will~Power Attack~Skill Focus|Perception~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="lead blades" s10="make whole" s13="versatile weapon" s16="fire shield/ (warm only)"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, you gain the form and flame of the ‹monster/salamander› and the following powers.

::ab[Serpent's Tail (Su)]{l=1 icon=power passive="Your legs twist together into a slithering serpentine tail. Your speed is reduced by 10 feet (to a minimum of 5 feet), and you can't be ‹rule/trip«ped›. Your footwear melds into your body and is non-functional unless it provides a constant bonus and does not need to be activated. You also gain a tail slap ‹umr/natural attack› that deals 1d6 points of damage (1d4 if you are Small) + your Strength modifier." imp4="Your speed is no longer reduced by 10 feet." imp8="Your tail slap's damage increases to 1d8 (1d6 if you are Small) + your Strength modifier." imp12="Your reach with your tail slap increases by 5 feet."}

::ab[Salamander Scales (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="You gain fire ‹umr/resistance› 5 and a +1 natural armor bonus to AC." imp8="Your fire resistance increases to 10 and your natural armor bonus increases to +2." imp16="Your fire resistance increases to 20 and your natural armor bonus increases to +3."}

::ab[Bloodsmith (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can choose one manufactured weapon you wield, shield you wield, or piece of armor you wear to gain the benefit of ‹spell/greater magic weapon› (weapon) or ‹spell/magic vestment› (shield or armor). The caster level of this effect is equal to your bloodrager level, and its effects end when your bloodrage ends or when you no longer wear or wield the weapon, shield, or armor."}

::ab[Scorching Heat (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee passive="Your weapons conduct heat. You deal 1d6 additional points of fire damage with natural weapons and melee attacks made with metal weapons. You also deal 2d6 points of fire damage per round to any creature you ‹rule/grapple›. This does not damage your equipment."}
::ab[Master Bloodsmith (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost ability="You can affect up to two items simultaneously with your *bloodsmith* bloodline power." imp20="You can affect up to three items."}

::ab[Essence of Fire (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You become ‹umr/immune› to fire damage. In addition, your bloodrager damage reduction increases by 5. If you don't have bloodrager damage reduction, you gain DR 10/magic instead. You have these benefits constantly, even while you are not bloodraging."}

shadow.name: Shadow
description: ## Shadow Bloodline
‹SOURCE Blood of Shadows/16›
Because of your heritage, you can move and see through shadows as if you are part of them. Your fury absorbs light, warmth, and strength.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Blind-Fight~Combat Reflexes~Improved Initiative~Lightning Reflexes~Quick Draw~Step Up"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="ray of enfeeblement" s10=darkvision s13="deeper darkness" s16="shadow conjuration"}
### Bloodline Powers
Your bloodline gives you powers over light and darkness.

::ab[Shadow Vision (Su)]{l=1 icon=power passive="While bloodraging, you gain low-light vision if you don't already have it. If you already have low-light vision, you gain darkvision to a distance of 30 feet." imp10="You gain darkvision with a range of 30 feet, or add 30 feet to the range of your darkvision if you already have it."}

::ab[Shades of Rage (Su)]{l=4 icon=aura passive="Whenever you enter a bloodrage, the ‹rule/light level› within 30 feet of you decreases by one step. Natural light is always affected, but magical illumination dispels this ability if the caster of the light effect succeeds at a caster level check against a DC equal to 10 + your class level."}

::ab[Strength of Shadows (Su)]{l=8 icon=def-melee passive="You gain cold ‹umr/resistance› 10. Your melee attacks deal additional cold damage equal to the critical multiplier of the weapon you use." imp13="Your cold resistance increases to 20." imp18="You become ‹umr/immune› to cold damage."}

::ab[Strike Through Shadow (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee ability="You can declare a single melee attack as a strike through shadow. This attack moves through its target's shadow and strikes from below or inside its armor. You make this attack against the target's touch AC." useInc="3~bloodrager~12"}

::ab[Shadow Door (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="You can take any part of your movement through shadows, teleporting to another location within 60 feet. This ability functions as ‹spell/dimension door›, except you can't use shadow door in brightly lit areas. You can teleport in this manner a total of 10 feet per class level each day."}

::ab[Shadow Warrior (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-boost passive="You become shadow and fury personified. You see perfectly in natural and magical darkness. Whenever you deal hit point damage with a spell or attack, you also deal 2 points of ‹rule/Strength damage› to each creature that took damage."}

shapechanger.name: Shapechanger
description: ## Shapechanger Bloodline
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/20›
The blood of ‹monster/doppelganger«s›, ‹monster/faceless stalker«s›, ‹template/lycanthrope«s›, or other shapechangers courses through your veins.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Dodge~Fleet~Improved Initiative~Improved Unarmed Strike~Lightning Reflexes~Power Attack~Weapon Focus"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="enlarge person" s10="alter self" s13=fly s16=stoneskin}
### Bloodline Powers
Transformative powers in your blood emerge when you bloodrage.

::ab[Shifting Aspect (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="You gain the benefit of the ‹feat/Aspect of the Beast› feat while bloodraging. As part of the action to enter a bloodrage, you can choose any one of the four manifestations to affect you for the duration of your bloodrage."}

::ab[Spontaneous Change (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic swift="When entering a bloodrage, you can cast a transmutation spell that affects only you, provided that the spell's normal casting time is 1 round or shorter. The spell lasts for as long as you continue bloodraging, regardless of the spell's normal duration. You can use this ability only to cast bloodrager spells you know."}

::ab[Evolving Aspect (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="You gain *Aspect of the Beast* as a bonus feat even if you don't meet the prerequisites. As normal for the feat, you must choose one of the manifestations, and you gain its benefits even while not bloodraging. When you enter a bloodrage, you can choose a second manifestation to affect you simultaneously for the duration of your bloodrage."}

::ab[Beastskin (Su)]{l=12 icon=def-melee ability="When entering a bloodrage, you can cause your skin to grow thicker and more bestial. Your ‹rule/damage reduction› from your bloodrager levels increases by 2, but it can be bypassed with silver weapons. Your natural attacks (including unarmed strikes) are then treated as silver for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction."}

::ab[Shed Skin (Su)]{l=16 icon=def immediate="When your bloodrage ends, you can choose one spell or spell-like ability affecting you and attempt to shake it off. Attempt a dispel check (1d20 + your bloodrager level) as if you were casting ‹spell/dispel magic›. If you succeed, the effect is dispelled."}

::ab[True Shapechanger (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-boost passive="You are immune to all transmutation spells and effects unless you are a willing target. You can also use ‹spell/greater polymorph› at will as a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to your bloodrager level targeting only yourself. These effects function even while you're not bloodraging."}

sphinx.name: Sphinx
description: ## Sphinx Bloodline
‹SOURCE Heroes of Golarion/23›

Those who successfully traded riddles with sphinxes sometimes find themselves or their descendants exhibiting both arcane power and violent fury. These bloodragers find being outwitted or outmaneuvered especially infuriating; though they are usually scholarly and erudite, their fits of frenzied rage are all the more terrifying in comparison.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Alertness~Combat Casting~Dazzling Display~Improved Critical~Iron Will~Rending Fury~Skill Focus|Intimidate~Voice of the Sibyl"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="divine favor" s10="touch of idiocy" s13="searing light" s16="bestow curse"}
### Bloodline Powers
You gain the ruthless claws and implacable voice of the ‹family/sphinx› while bloodraging.

::ab[Claws (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee passive="You grow claws while bloodraging. These claws are treated as ‹umr/natural weapons›, allowing you to make two claw attacks as a full attack, using your full base attack bonus. These attacks deal 1d6 points of damage each (1d4 if you are Small) plus your Strength modifier." imp4="Your claws are considered magic weapons for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction." imp8="The base damage your claws deal increases to 1d8 points (1d6 if you are small)." imp12="The critical threat range for your claws increases to 19-20."}

::ab[Roar (Su)]{l=4 icon=lower useMod3=Cha standard="The bloodrager learns the secrets of the sphinx's roar. He can roar as a standard action a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier. Enemies within 60 feet of the bloodrager that can hear the roar must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the bloodrager's character level + his Charisma modifier) or become ‹misc/frightened› for 1d6 rounds. Whether or not the save is successful, a creature cannot be the target of the bloodrager's roar again for 24 hours." imp10="The bloodrager can instead cause targets who fail their save to be ‹misc/panicked› or ‹misc/stunned› (bloodrager's choice) and ‹misc/deafened› for the duration." imp16="The bloodrager can instead cause targets who fail their save to be ‹rule/paralyzed› for 1 round."}

::ab[Desert Fortitude (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to electricity and fire. In addition, you gain ‹spell/endure elements› as a constant spell-like ability and a +2 competence bonus on saves against arcane spells." imp6="Your resistance to electricity and fire increases to 10."}

::ab[Rending Rage (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee ability="You gain a ‹umr/rend› attack, as the universal monster rule, that deals extra damage equal to 2d4 + 1-1/2 times your Strength modifier." imp18="This damage increases to 2d6 + 1-1/2 times your Strength modifier."}

::ab[Master of Mysteries (Su)]{l=16 icon=magic-def standard="You can cast either ‹spell/maze› or ‹spell/symbol of insanity› once per day as a spell-like ability, with a caster level equal to your character level. You also gain spell resistance equal to 11 + your level."}

::ab[Final Riddle (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to fire and electricity and ignore environmental penalties due to temperature. Your competence bonus on saving throws against arcane spells increases to +6. You have these benefits constantly, even while not raging."}

undead.name: Undead
description: ## Undead Bloodline
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/22›

The foul corruption of undeath is a part of you. Somewhere in the past, death became infused with your lineage. Your connection to the attributes of the undead bestows frightening power when your bloodrage.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Diehard~Dodge~Endurance~Intimidating Prowess~Iron Will~Mobility~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="chill touch" s10="false life" s13="vampiric touch" s16=enervation}
### Bloodline Powers
When you enter a bloodrage, the powers of undeath manifest, empowering your attacks with fear and your body with foul resistances.

::ab[Frightful Charger (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee ability="When you hit a creature with a ‹rule/charge› attack, that creature becomes ‹misc/shaken› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your bloodrager level (minimum 1). This effect does not cause an existing shaken or frightened condition (from this ability or another source) to turn into frightened or panicked. This is a mind-affecting fear effect."}
::ab[Ghost Strike (Su)]{l=4 icon=melee passive="Your melee attacks are treated as if they have the ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› weapon special ability."}
::ab[Death's Gift (Su)]{l=8 icon=def passive="You gain cold ‹umr/resistance› 10, as well as DR 10/- against nonlethal damage."}

::ab[Frightful Strikes (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee usage="Once per bloodrage." swift="You can empower your melee attacks with fear. For 1 round, creatures you hit with your melee attacks become shaken. Creatures who are already shaken become ‹misc/frightened›. This is a mind-affecting fear effect." imp16="Those who are already frightened become ‹misc/panicked›."}

::ab[Incorporeal Bloodrager (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost usage="Once a day." ability="You can choose to become ‹umr/incorporeal›. You take only half damage from magic corporeal sources, and you take no damage from non-magic weapons and objects. Your attacks deal damage as normal due to your *ghost strike* bloodrage power."}

::ab[One Foot in the Grave (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to cold, nonlethal damage, paralysis, and sleep. The DR from your damage reduction ability increases to 8. Unintelligent undead don't notice you unless you attack them. You gain a +4 morale bonus on saving throws made against spells and spell-like abilities cast by undead. You have these benefits constantly, even while not bloodraging."}

verdant.name: Verdant
description: ## Verdant Bloodline
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/17›
Your body is suffused with raw plant life. When you bloodrage, your plant-like characteristics bolster your durability and grant you some control over vegetation.
::list[Bonus Feats]{link=feat all="Bolstered Resilience~Diehard~Endurance~Great Fortitude~Power Attack~Raging Vitality~Toughness"}
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7=entangle s10=greensight s13="burst of nettles" s16="command plants"}
### Bloodline Powers
While bloodraging, you gain plant-like abilities and immunities as you become overgrown with jagged branches, rugged bark, and thick vines.

::ab[Verdant Growth (Su)]{l=1 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/fast healing› 1 while bloodraging. If you have a bloodrager class feature that also grants you fast healing (such as the ‹arc-bloodrager/spelleater«'s› blood of life class feature), you can stack up to half of your fast healing from such abilities with the fast healing gained from this ability, but you cannot do so by an amount that exceeds your Charisma bonus (minimum 0)." imp4="You instead gain fast healing 2." imp7="You instead gain fast healing 3." imp10="You instead gain fast healing 4." imp13="You instead gain fast healing 5." imp16="You instead gain fast healing 6." imp19="You instead gain fast healing 7."}

::ab[Oaken Skin (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="When entering a bloodrage, your skin thickens like bark and you gain a +2 enhancement bonus to your natural armor." imp8="The bonus becomes +3." imp12="The bonus becomes +4." imp16="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6."}
::ab[Botanical Plasticity (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost swift="You can bend and twist the plant life within your body to elongate your limbs. Your reach increases by 5 feet until the end of your turn."}

::ab[Verdant Call (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic swift="When entering a bloodrage, you can animate nearby plants to aid you in combat, functioning as ‹spell/wilderness soldiers›, using your Charisma modifier instead of your Wisdom modifier. This effect lasts for as long as you continue bloodraging, regardless of the spell's normal duration."}

::ab[Nature's Thorns (Su)]{l=16 icon=lower passive="Creatures that hit you in melee (other than those using manufactured reach weapons) take 4d6 points of piercing damage. A creature takes this damage only on its first successful attack against you in a combat round, and not on subsequent attacks in that round."}

::ab[Verdant Apotheosis (Su)]{l=20 icon=power-def passive="Verdant power surges throughout your being. While bloodraging, you gain ‹umr/tremorsense› out to 30 feet. In addition, you gain immunity to paralysis, poison, sleep, and stunning effects, even while you are not bloodraging."}

vestige.name: Vestige
description: ## Vestige Bloodline
‹SOURCE Blood of the Ancients/4›
You are the descendant of great heroes from a lost civilization.

**Bonus Feats:** ‹feat/Combat Casting›, ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency›, ‹feat/Greater Weapon Focus›\*, ‹feat/Greater Weapon Specialization›\*, ‹feat/Iron Will›, ‹feat/Weapon Focus›, ‹feat/Weapon Specialization›\*.
The bloodrager counts his total bloodrager levels as fighter levels for the purpose of qualifying for any feats marked with an asterisk (\*). If he also has levels in fighter, these levels stack.
::ab[Bonus Spells by Bloodrager Level]{icon=learn s7="true strike" s10="false life" s13="phantom steed" s16="mass enlarge person"}
### Bloodline Powers
When you bloodrage, you transform into an ancestor or another notable figure from a lost civilization.

::ab[Warrior's Discipline (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost swift="You tap into untold generations of battle experience to control your battle fury. You can halve any morale bonuses gained from bloodrage to be able to use Intelligence- or Charisma-based skills, or any abilities requiring patience or concentration. This effect lasts for 1 round."}

::ab[Ancient Tactics (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="While you are bloodraging, whenever you or an ally within 30 feet of you would gain a bonus on attack rolls or to AC due to battlefield position (such as ‹rule/flanking› or ‹rule/cover›), that bonus is increased by 1. Allies must be able to see and hear you to gain this bonus. You can expend a spell slot as an immediate action to further increase this bonus by 1 per level of the spell slot expended until the beginning of your next turn."}

::ab[Legacy Style (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="Select any one ‹main/style feat›. You can use this style feat while bloodraging; however, if you do not meet all of the style feat's prerequisites, you must expend a spell slot when entering the style's stance, and the benefits last for only 1 round per level of the spell slot expended or for the duration of your bloodrage, whichever is shorter. If you already have this style feat, you can enter the style's stance as part of the free action to bloodrage."}

::ab[War's Memory (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic-lower standard="You can manifest an illusion of an ancestor's bloody battlefield once per day. This functions as ‹spell/hallucinatory terrain›, except it can create only battlefields drawn from a civilization's past, and the effect ends when your bloodrage ends. The saving throw to disbelieve is equal to 10 + 1/2 your bloodrager level + your Constitution modifier. The horrifying scene causes creatures within the area of effect to take a -2 penalty on saves versus fear effects until they disbelieve the illusion. If a creature fails the Will save to disbelieve the illusion, it gains the ‹misc/shaken› condition until your bloodrage ends, or until it leaves the area of effect. This is an illusion (glamer) and fear effect."}

::ab[Legacy Conduit (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost ability="When using the *legacy style* bloodline power, you can expend a spell slot as a swift action to share the benefits of the selected style feat with all allies within 30 feet. The benefits last for 1 round per level of the spell slot expended. Allies must still enter the style's stance as a swift action."}

::ab[Call to Arms (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic usage="Once per day." standard="You can summon a ghostly army from a fallen civilization once per day. This functions as ‹spell/spiritual ally› with a caster level equal to your level, except that it summons a number of allies equal to your Constitution modifier, and the allies use your Strength modifier for attack rolls. You can direct all of these allies with a single swift action. Each ally can attack a different target or the same target, as you direct. The allies remain until the spell's duration ends or your bloodrage ends, whichever comes first."}
